blurb:
When the intake team found Otis at the county shelter, he was so stressed from the noise and chaos that he was urinating on himself. But the minute they got him into a quiet room, he buried his head in someone's lap and just melted. He wasn't a problem dog. He was just scared.

Now that he's had time to decompress at Our Pal's Place, the real Otis has shown up. He remembers his house training, sits politely when asked, and wags his tail like he means it. He loves attention and wants nothing more than to make his humans happy. The volunteers say he's turned into a fun-loving guy who clearly just needed to get out of that overwhelming environment.

This is the kind of dog who's going to be grateful every single day that you chose him. He was literally scheduled to be euthanized just because of timing, not because anything was wrong with him. Someone is going to get an incredibly loyal companion who knows what it's like to need rescuing.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. Call or email to set up a meet and greet with Otis.
scoring_notes:
• Life-or-death rescue story with dramatic transformation appeals to everyone
• Well-behaved, house-trained dog ready for immediate placement
• Scheduled for euthanization creates urgent call-to-action for readers

blurb:
After years in his home, Junior's family suddenly decided they were allergic to him. That was their story anyway. So Junior ended up at the county shelter, watching people walk by his kennel day after day. Black dog syndrome is real, and it was working against him.

But here's what makes Junior special: this dog has figured out the good life. His favorite volunteer Katie takes him on ice cream dates to Bruster's and Pelicans. After their outings, he goes to her house and acts like the perfect houseguest. House trained, well mannered, just happy to be included. He knows how to live in a home because he did it successfully for years.

Junior prefers to be the only dog and does best with older kids, but if you're looking for a loyal companion who will never take you for granted, this is your guy. He's already proven he can be the perfect family member. He just needs someone who won't change their mind about him.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. Call or email to meet Junior and maybe plan your own ice cream date.
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking abandonment story highlights loyalty versus human fickleness
• Proven family dog with house training and perfect manners
• Black dog syndrome and long wait time create urgency

blurb:
Midna and her brother Frankenwhisker came to Good Mews from a county shelter together. She's very scared in new environments and will need someone who understands that and can give her space and patience to build her confidence. She's not going to warm up overnight, but she will settle in with time.

She's bonded with Frankenwhisker and they love to wrestle and play together. They have to be adopted as a pair, which actually works in your favor. Two cats are usually easier than one, and having her brother there will help her feel more secure as she adjusts to a new home.

Midna needs someone who won't rush her and can let her come around on her own timeline. If you've got the patience and space for a bonded pair, these two will eventually make great companions once they settle in.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet Midna and Frankenwhisker together.
scoring_notes:
• Bonded pair story appeals to readers who love sibling relationships
• Requires adopting two cats and significant patience limiting appeal
• Brother-sister duo waiting together tugs at heartstrings

blurb:
Luke's story starts with loss, but he doesn't know that part yet. His mom Miss Daisy and her two puppies ended up at the county shelter when their owner passed away unexpectedly. No family member stepped up to help. The boyfriend just dropped them off and walked away. The county held them for weeks hoping someone from the family would come, but nobody did.

Now that Luke is safe at Our Pal's Place, his real personality is coming out. He's a fun-loving, playful boy who got to experience his first summer camp when he arrived during Camp O.P.P. Kids walked him on trails and gave him all the attention a young shepherd mix needs. He's got the face of a Belgian Malinois but the smaller build of his German Shepherd mom.

Luke is still young and ready to bond with his new family. He's been through enough change for one year. What he needs now is someone who's in it for the long haul, ready to give him the stability he's been missing.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. Call or email to meet Luke and see if you're the family he's been waiting for.
scoring_notes:
• Tragic backstory of family loss and abandonment creates emotional connection
• Young, adaptable dog ready to bond with new family
• Camp experience shows his sweet personality and potential

blurb:
His name is Pork Tenderloin and he's a little misunderstood. He came to Good Mews from a county shelter for a better shot at finding the right home. He's sweet, but he likes attention on his own terms. When he's in the mood for affection, he'll come find you.

Pork Tenderloin gets overwhelmed more quickly than some cats, so he needs a calm environment and someone who won't push. He appreciates a patient approach and space when he needs it. He's not going to be the cat who's immediately in your lap, but that doesn't mean he's not loving.

He'll do best with someone who's cat-savvy and understands feline body language. If you can read his signals and respect his boundaries, he has the potential to form a really special bond. He just needs the right person who gets him.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. Submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet Pork Tenderloin.
scoring_notes:
• Unforgettable name guarantees reader attention and social media buzz
• Needs patient, cat-savvy adopter limiting potential audience
• Misunderstood shelter cat waiting for the right person to see his worth

blurb:
Beignet was adopted from Good Mews as a kitten, but somehow ended up scared and alone at a county shelter. Good Mews brought her back when they found out, and she's been in foster care getting her confidence back. Now she's ready for round two.

She warmed up quickly in her foster home and showed her sweet personality. She loves being petted and is a social eater who likes company during meals. When she wants attention, she'll sit close or lean against you. She can give gentle love bites when she gets overstimulated, so you'll want to learn her signals and respect when she's had enough.

Beignet needs someone who understands that cats communicate through body language and knows when to give space. If you can read those cues, she's going to be a loving companion who just had a rough patch.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet Beignet.
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking second-chance story of abandonment and resilience
• Social, affectionate cat who just needs understanding adopters
• Foster success shows she's ready for permanent loving home
